findings.evidence: `rg -n "show MonoidAlgebra.single x r"` hits line 89; the nearby existing scalar proof in `RootsOfUnity.lean:159` documents the analogous `show` rewrite.
findings.fix: Either add the same kind of explanatory comment used for the generator scalar lemma in `RootsOfUnity.lean`, or replace the inline `show` with an explicit named rewrite such as `MonoidAlgebra.smul_single`/`smul_single'` so the proof does not rely on an opaque `simp` subgoal.
findings.issue: `pointsMulEquiv_symm_apply_single_zmod_val_smul` rewrites through `show MonoidAlgebra.single x r = r • MonoidAlgebra.single x (1 : R) by simp` without documenting why this `show` is needed.

summary: One scalar-normal-form proof uses an undocumented inline `show`, which this proof-quality rubric treats as brittle unless the coercion/rewrite obstruction is explained or avoided.
